status_notes: Initial research from AWS S3 documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs) and partial Pass 2 (API). Needs Pass 3 (cross-reference) and Pass 5 (human review with live AWS environment).
notes: Object storage. Most commonly misconfigured AWS service. Security surface includes encryption (4 types), access controls (bucket policies, ACLs, block public access, object ownership), data protection (versioning, object lock, MFA delete), and comprehensive monitoring.

match_nodes.description: Server-side encryption configuration for S3 buckets

data.description: Server-side encryption applied automatically to all new objects. Options: SSE-S3 (AES256, default since January 2023), SSE-KMS (AWS KMS key), DSSE-KMS (dual-layer KMS encryption). SSE-C (customer-provided keys) is being phased out and disabled for new buckets from April 2026.

options.name: SSE-S3 (AES256)
options.description: Amazon S3-managed keys. Default since January 2023. No additional cost.

options.name: SSE-KMS
options.description: AWS KMS customer managed or AWS managed key. Provides audit trail via CloudTrail. KMS API costs apply.

options.name: DSSE-KMS
options.description: Dual-layer server-side encryption with KMS keys. Two layers of encryption for compliance requirements.

options.name: SSE-C (deprecated for new buckets)
options.description: Customer-provided encryption keys. Being phased out — disabled for new buckets from April 2026.

default.note: All new buckets have SSE-S3 (AES256) encryption enabled by default since January 2023.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key for audit trail and key rotation control. SSE-S3 is acceptable for basic encryption requirements.

match_nodes.description: Account and bucket-level settings preventing public access to S3 resources

data.description: Four independent settings that block public access at the account or bucket level. Can be applied at account level (affects all buckets) or per-bucket. Account-level settings override bucket-level settings.

options.description: Rejects PUT requests that include public ACLs
Ignores all public ACLs on the bucket and its objects
Rejects bucket policy changes that would make the bucket public
Restricts access to buckets with public policies to AWS service principals and authorized users only

default.note: All four settings enabled by default for new buckets and new accounts.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable all 4 settings at the account level. Only disable at the bucket level for specific use cases (static website hosting).

match_nodes.description: JSON resource-based policies on S3 buckets

data.description: JSON resource-based policies attached directly to S3 buckets. Primary access control mechanism for S3. Define who (Principal) can perform what actions (Action) on which resources (Resource) under what conditions (Condition). Support cross-account access without IAM roles.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use bucket policies as the primary access control mechanism. Combine with Block Public Access. Use conditions to enforce encryption in transit (aws:SecureTransport) and at rest.

match_nodes.description: Controls ACL usage and object ownership for S3 buckets

data.description: Controls whether ACLs can be used and who owns objects uploaded to the bucket. Three settings determine ACL behavior.

options.description: ACLs disabled. Bucket owner automatically owns all objects. Recommended setting.
Bucket owner owns objects uploaded with bucket-owner-full-control ACL.
Uploading account owns the object. ACLs are active.

default.note: Default for new buckets. ACLs are disabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use BucketOwnerEnforced to disable ACLs entirely. Manage access through bucket policies and IAM policies instead.

match_nodes.description: S3 bucket versioning to maintain multiple object versions

data.description: Keeps multiple variants of an object in the same bucket. Once enabled, versioning cannot be fully disabled — only suspended. Protects against accidental deletion and overwrites.

options.description: Default. No versioning.
All object versions retained.
New versions not created but existing versions retained.

default.note: Versioning is disabled by default. Cannot be turned off once enabled — only suspended.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable versioning for data protection. Required for Object Lock and cross-region replication.

match_nodes.description: WORM (Write Once Read Many) protection for S3 objects

data.description: Prevents objects from being deleted or overwritten for a fixed amount of time or indefinitely. Two retention modes available. Requires versioning to be enabled. Must be enabled at bucket creation time.

options.name: Governance Mode
options.description: Users with special permissions (s3:BypassGovernanceRetention) can override retention settings or delete objects.

options.name: Compliance Mode
options.description: No one can delete or overwrite protected objects, including the root account. Retention period cannot be shortened.

default.note: Object Lock is not enabled by default. Must be enabled at bucket creation.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Compliance mode for regulatory requirements where data must not be deleted. Use Governance mode for testing or when administrative override is needed.

configure.note: Object Lock can only be enabled during bucket creation. Use --object-lock-enabled-for-bucket with create-bucket.

match_nodes.description: Require MFA to permanently delete object versions

data.description: Adds an additional layer of protection by requiring MFA authentication to permanently delete object versions or change the versioning state of a bucket. Requires versioning to be enabled. Can only be enabled by the root account.

default.note: MFA Delete is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable MFA Delete on critical buckets to prevent accidental or malicious permanent deletion of versioned objects.
data.limits: Can only be enabled/disabled by the root account using the CLI or API. Cannot be configured via console.

audit.note: Check the MFADelete field in the response.

configure.note: Must be run as root account. Cannot be enabled via console or CloudFormation.

match_nodes.description: Server access logging for S3 buckets

data.description: Records detailed requests made to a bucket and delivers log files to a target S3 bucket. Logs include requester, bucket name, request time, action, response status, and error code. Best-effort delivery (not guaranteed).

default.note: Server access logging is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable access logging for security auditing and access analysis. Consider CloudTrail data events as an alternative for guaranteed delivery.

match_nodes.description: Enforce HTTPS (TLS) for S3 bucket access

data.description: Enforce encrypted connections to S3 by adding a bucket policy condition that denies requests not using HTTPS. Minimum TLS version is configurable.
data.vendor_recommendation: Add a bucket policy condition denying requests where aws:SecureTransport is false. Set minimum TLS version to 1.2.

audit.note: Check bucket policy for a Deny statement with condition aws:SecureTransport: false.

configure.note: Add a statement to the bucket policy: {"Effect":"Deny","Principal":"*","Action":"s3:*","Resource":["arn:aws:s3:::{bucket}","arn:aws:s3:::{bucket}/*"],"Condition":{"Bool":{"aws:SecureTransport":"false"}}}

match_nodes.description: Private S3 access from VPC without internet gateway

data.description: Enable private connectivity from VPC to S3 without requiring an internet gateway or NAT device. Two types: Gateway endpoint (free, route table entry) and Interface endpoint (ENI with private IP, PrivateLink).

options.name: Gateway Endpoint
options.description: Free. Route table-based. S3 and DynamoDB only. Most common.

options.name: Interface Endpoint (PrivateLink)
options.description: ENI with private IP. Per-hour and per-GB charges. Supports on-premises access via VPN/Direct Connect.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Gateway endpoints for most workloads (free). Use Interface endpoints when access from on-premises via VPN/Direct Connect is required.

match_nodes.description: Named network endpoints with dedicated access policies for S3

data.description: Named network endpoints attached to buckets, each with its own access policy. Simplify managing data access at scale by creating application-specific entry points with tailored permissions. Can be restricted to VPC-only access.

match_nodes.description: Cross-region and same-region replication for S3 objects

data.description: Automatic, asynchronous copying of objects across S3 buckets. Supports cross-region replication (CRR) for disaster recovery and compliance, and same-region replication (SRR) for aggregation and backup. Requires versioning enabled on both source and destination buckets.

options.name: Cross-Region Replication
options.description: Replicates objects to a bucket in a different AWS Region.

options.name: Same-Region Replication
options.description: Replicates objects to a bucket in the same AWS Region.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use CRR for disaster recovery and compliance with data residency requirements. Ensure versioning is enabled on both buckets.

match_nodes.description: S3 Inventory for auditing encryption, replication status across objects

data.description: Scheduled reports listing objects and their metadata including encryption status, replication status, storage class, and more. Output to CSV, ORC, or Parquet format in a destination bucket. Runs daily or weekly.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use S3 Inventory to audit encryption status, identify unencrypted objects, and verify replication compliance across all objects in a bucket.

match_nodes.description: Organization-wide S3 storage analytics and best practice recommendations

data.description: Cloud storage analytics that provides organization-wide visibility into object storage usage, activity trends, and actionable recommendations. Identifies buckets without best practices (encryption, versioning, replication). Available at account or AWS Organizations level.

match_nodes.description: Automatic object transition, expiration, and cleanup rules

data.description: Rules that automatically transition objects between storage classes, expire objects, or clean up incomplete multipart uploads. Security-relevant uses: delete incomplete multipart uploads (which can accumulate and incur cost), expire noncurrent object versions, and enforce data retention policies.
data.vendor_recommendation: Configure lifecycle rules to: (1) abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days, (2) expire noncurrent versions after a defined retention period, (3) transition infrequently accessed data to cheaper storage classes.
